title: stachys subcrenata var. fragilis
intro: Stachys subcrenata var. fragilis, also known as the fragrant hedge nettle or fragrant woundwort, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as wound healing, relief from respiratory issues, and protection against infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Stachys subcrenata var. fragilis are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to promote wound healing, soothe respiratory issues, and boost the immune system.
